---
title: "Governance"
subtitle: "The standard, the record of decisions, and the tests that keep it all honest"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Governance <span class="sep">›</span> Overview
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This section is how the lab governs itself.** It holds the rules, the record of past choices, and
the automated checks.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-grade-b no-dot">Process is enforced</span>
<span class="pc-next">[Read the standard](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md)</span>
:::
:::

Governance answers "by what authority?" for everything else on the site. It has three pillars: the
**standard** that defines how strategy is reasoned about, the **decision records** that capture why
the project is built the way it is, and the **testing standard** that guards every calculation.

::: {.cil-cards}
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">The standard</div>
<h3>Commercial Intelligence Specification</h3>
<p>The normative document: lifecycle, ontology, evidence model, confidence grades, and mandatory
rules. This is the technical standard itself.</p>
<span class="card-go">Read the CIS</span>
[](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md){.stretched-link aria-label="Open the CIS"}
:::

::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">The record</div>
<h3>Decision Records</h3>
<p>Append-only Architecture Decision Records — why the lab uses this architecture and why CIS governs
it.</p>
<span class="card-go">Read the ADRs</span>
[](../docs/decision-records/index.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Decision Records"}
:::

::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">The guardrail</div>
<h3>Testing Standard</h3>
<p>What the automated test suite guarantees, what it covers, and how to run it.</p>
<span class="card-go">See the tests</span>
[](testing-standard.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Testing Standard"}
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
**Two views of the CIS, deliberately distinct.** The version here in Governance is the **standard**
itself. The [Working Paper's CIS chapter](../working-paper/03-commercial-intelligence-specification.qmd)
is a shorter **discussion of how the standard is applied** to this project. Read the standard for the
rules; read the chapter for the application.
:::
